summary: Ran full end-to-end frontend UI testing of Project Wizard (frontend-only, localStorage-only). Verified: dashboard empty state, template picker (2 available + 10 non-clickable Coming Soon), project creation for both Business Website and Portfolio, three-panel Wizard layout, section navigation, every question type (text, textarea, select, multiselect, toggle, color, chips-add, files), live DNA panel updates, Project Health % updates, builder recommendation auto-switching (Booking link -> emergent) and manual override (dna-builder-lovable/emergent), context file upload with library entry, localStorage persistence across page reload, Outputs page with 4 tabs (emergentPrompt / lovablePrompt / markdownSpec / qaChecklist), content contains business fields (name, tagline, description, goal, CTA), Download + Regenerate + Copy buttons, Launch checklist with per-task toggles, progress %, strikethrough on complete, and 'Mark as launched' behaviour, and the Launched status chip on dashboard card. All flows work. Tested at 1440x900 and 1024x768 (tablet) — no layout regressions observed. Design polish is high (Linear/Notion-quality).

integration_issues.issue: The Copy button label flashes 'Copied' visually (screenshots + code confirm) but on click via Playwright in headless Chromium the label sometimes still reads 'Copy' after ~500ms — this appears to be a headless clipboard permission quirk (navigator.clipboard.writeText may fail silently). Toast handling still triggers 'Copied to clipboard' when clipboard access is granted. Not a code bug per se; consider adding a fallback (execCommand or a document.execCommand('copy') on a hidden textarea) to make copy reliable across environments.

action_items: Optional: harden clipboard copy with an execCommand fallback so headless / non-secure contexts still show the 'Copied' state reliably.
Optional: consider adding aria-current='page' to the active sidebar section for accessibility (currently only visual styling communicates active state).
Optional (code-review): React Router v6 shows two future-flag warnings in console (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath) — opt-in flags on BrowserRouter would silence them.
critical_code_review_comments: DNA mapping uses `Name: dna?.business.name || dna?.profile.fullName` inside the 'Business' block for both Business and Portfolio templates. For portfolio projects, the DNA panel therefore labels the block 'Business' with the person's name — semantically slightly off. Consider a separate 'Profile' block for portfolio templates (or template-aware DNA block titles).

computeRecommendedBuilder ignores `template.builderRules.emergentSignals` — the switching to Emergent is hard-coded to features ['Booking link','Live chat']. Signals in builderRules are defined but unused. Not a bug for current templates but reduces the value of the data-driven contract.
Wizard.jsx side-effect of `updateProject` on every keystroke of the project name input writes to localStorage on every character — fine for MVP but consider debouncing for larger docs.
Outputs.jsx download extension is always '.md' via an unnecessary ternary (`active === 'markdownSpec' ? 'md' : active === 'qaChecklist' ? 'md' : 'md'`) — simplify.

In Wizard.jsx the right aside uses `absolute lg:static top-14` with `z-20`; at md widths (768–1024) the panel can be toggled with the DNA toggle button; verify overlay behaviour on real tablets — visually OK in this test.
seed_data_creation: None — tests created 'TEST Riverstone Studios' and 'TEST Alex Chen Portfolio' via UI. Cleared via UI delete during flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is frontend-only (localStorage key 'projectWizard.v1.projects'). Use page.evaluate("localStorage.clear()") before each run to hit the empty dashboard state. All critical data-testids exist and are stable. Templates: 'business-website' (8 sections) and 'portfolio' (7 sections). Builder recommendation switches to 'emergent' when features include 'Booking link' or 'Live chat'. Copy button uses navigator.clipboard.writeText — headless environments may not grant clipboard permission by default; use context.grant_permissions(['clipboard-read','clipboard-write']) if strict copy verification is needed.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing test cases. Only advisory notes above.
